description_long: This object is a roughly hewn stone block with a stylized, sculpted face on one side, featuring deep-set eyes, a pronounced nose, and a wide, angular mouth. The stone has a weathered, gray surface with visible texturing and irregularities, giving it an ancient and monumental appearance. The top of the block is hollowed out, forming a square-shaped cavity, suggesting it could serve as a planter or receptacle. The facial features are geometric and abstract, reminiscent of Moai statues or other megalithic art. The block's sides are uneven, with chiseled facets and a rugged finish, while the interior cavity appears dark and deep. The overall impression is of a decorative or symbolic urban barrier, possibly used in parks, plazas, or themed public spaces.
description: A weathered gray stone block with a sculpted face and a hollowed-out top, featuring rough, chiseled surfaces and geometric facial features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the prominent sculpted face with deep-set eyes, angular nose, and wide mouth.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the block's right side and the depth of the facial features.
description_view_2: Another frontal view similar to view 0, emphasizing the symmetry and depth of the facial carving.
description_view_3: View highlighting the left side of the block, showing the angular cheek and the hollowed top more clearly.
attribute_location_context: urban park or themed public plaza
attribute_installation_method: set on a level concrete or stone base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface appears rough and weathered, with irregular chisel marks and some darkening in recessed areas, suggesting exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: The block features a sculpted face as its primary decorative element; no additional markings or inscriptions are visible.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely used as a standalone decorative or functional barrier
